name: George Kurtz
role: Chief Executive Officer, President and Director
bio: George Kurtz co-founded CrowdStrike in 2011 and has served as CEO since inception. His compensation is dominated by equity, with cash salary held at $1M; FY24 included an outsized stock award figure tied to a multi-year PSU grant priced under a Monte-Carlo simulation. All Other Compensation includes Company-paid personal security and private aircraft usage tied to a Board-approved security program.

footnotes: Stock Awards reflect grant-date fair value of RSUs and PSUs under FASB ASC Topic 718 — an accounting figure, not realized pay. Mr. Kurtz's multi-year PSU award is valued via Monte-Carlo simulation; the maximum-performance value of his FY25 PSU grants would be $31,850,681.
Non-Equity Incentive reflects the cash bonus paid under the Corporate Incentive Plan (CIP) for fiscal 2025 performance.

All Other Compensation includes ~$104,279 for personal security at residences and ~$898,426 for personal use of private aircraft, plus standard supplementary benefits (life/disability insurance, executive health, 401(k) match). These costs are tied to a Board-approved security program for Mr. Kurtz.
Stock Awards reflect grant-date fair value of RSUs and PSUs under FASB ASC Topic 718 — an accounting figure, not realized pay.
